stories.title: Travelers Championship R3 — Scheffler Leads at -16, Hovland Two Back

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Scheffler leads the Travelers at -16. Hovland 2 back at -14.

Scheffler shot a 60 in Round 2. Eleven birdies. One bogey.

They're paired together in Round 3 today. 🏌️

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
World No. 1 vs. world No. 2 at TPC River Highlands today.

Scheffler -16. Hovland -14. Paired together in Round 3.

Saturday golf doesn't get much better than this.

article.headline: Travelers Championship R3: Scheffler Leads at -16 as Hovland Hunts Him Down

stories.title: KPMG Women's PGA R3 — Korda Charges From -6 as History Awaits

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda is 6 back of the lead at the KPMG Women's PGA. Round 3 is today.

She already won the Chevron Championship and U.S. Women's Open this year.

One more major. History is waiting.

article.headline: KPMG Women's PGA R3: Korda Six Back With History Still in Reach

stories.title: Open Championship Preview — Royal Birkdale, Scheffler Defending

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
The Open Championship is July 16-19 at Royal Birkdale.

Scheffler is the defending champion. He's won 4 of the last 6 majors.

Three weeks away. The world No. 1 has unfinished business. 🏆

article.headline: Open Championship Preview: Royal Birkdale Awaits as Scheffler Prepares to Defend

stories.title: LIV Golf News — O'Neil and Rolapp Merger Talks, Norman Says It Doesn't Matter

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
LIV CEO and PGA Tour CEO have held merger talks.

Greg Norman's take: "doesn't matter anymore."

LIV is also switching from 54 to 72 holes to pursue world ranking points. A lot moving in professional golf.

article.headline: LIV Golf Status Report: Merger Talks, Norman's Shrug, and the 72-Hole Pivot

stories.title: Korda's Historic Chase — What Three Consecutive Majors Would Mean

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Only two players in LPGA history have won three consecutive majors in a calendar year.

Babe Zaharias in 1950. Inbee Park in 2013.

Nelly Korda is trying to join that list in 2026. She's 6 back with 2 rounds left.

article.headline: Nelly Korda and the Short List: What Three Consecutive LPGA Majors Would Mean
